Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Calendar Represents a scheduled game, match, or tournament instance —essentially, a time-bound event associated with a specific Field. The Calendar object anchors the gameplay timeline, enabling season-based, game-based, or tournament-based fan campaigns. Examples: UEFA Champions League Final : Madrid vs PSG, for a single event calendar. LaLiga 2025-2026 , including multiple matches, for the entire season calendar. new_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Calendar name (e.g. LaLiga 2025-26) new_calendar API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "new_calendar" , "parameters" : { "name" : "2025-26" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); new_calendar Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the calendarId , which will be used to link with related objects. Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354" } } list_calendars POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters list_calendars Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string Calendar Id list_calendars API Call Example list_calendars API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "list_calendars", "parameters": { "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "calendars": [ { "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "data": { "name": "2025-26" } } ] } } delete_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption calendarId string Then Calendar Id of the calendar to delete. delete_calendar API Call Example delete_calendar API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "delete_calendar", "parameters": { "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Cubes API for managing NFT zones within a Field. Each Cube represents a unique tokenized tile owned by fans, generated automatically when creating fields. Covers operations to list cubes with pagination and update cube pricing.

codeExamples.description: Lists cubes for a specific field with pagination limit, returning cube coordinates and IDs
Updates pricing for specific cubes identified by i_j coordinates to 5 USD (500 cents)
Example cube object showing coordinates, status, and dimensional data in centimeters

useCases: Fans purchase NFT cubes representing sections of a virtual sports field
Track cube ownership for reward distribution during live matches
Set dynamic pricing for premium field zones (near goals or centerfield)
Enable fans to own specific coordinates where game events occur
Generate revenue by selling tokenized field sections to supporters

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Field Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Call list_cubes with fieldId and limit parameters; use lastEvaluatedKey for pagination if the field has hundreds of cubes

implementationSteps.title: Set Cube Pricing
implementationSteps.description: Use update_cube-price with cubesList (i_j format) and priceInCents to establish pricing for specific zones

implementationSteps.title: Track Cube Status
implementationSteps.description: Monitor cube status (created, sold, active) and link cubeId to fan ownership for event-based rewards

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page list_cubes Request Body list_cubes API Call Example update_cube-price Request Body update_cube-price API Call Example Was this helpful? Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "cubes": [ { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "cubeId": "ffcab6f9-1e3f-49df-9d53-28b1cbc1df57", "cubeIndex": "2700_1800", "status": "created", "data": { "x": 2700, "y": 1800, "cubeLength": 300, "i": 9, "j": 6 } }, { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "cubeId": "ff86a9b3-ac68-456d-af12-2d4dc8622b94", "cubeIndex": "11400_1200", "status": "created", "data": { "x": 11400, "y": 1200, "cubeLength": 300, "i": 38, "j": 4 } }, { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "cubeId": "ff742a73-5cff-4f86-b8cd-afa8fc864f95", "cubeIndex": "9000_7500", "status": "created", "data": { "x": 9000, "y": 7500, "cubeLength": 300, "i": 30, "j": 25 } }, { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "cubeId": "ff2bec99-1e1c-479b-adb6-79775780eb56", "cubeIndex": "11400_3600", "status": "created", "data": { "x": 11400, "y": 3600, "cubeLength": 300, "i": 38, "j": 12 } }, { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "cubeId": "ff248cbb-e7ab-4510-817f-53f7d56cf3e0", "cubeIndex": "8400_7200", "status": "created", "data": { "x": 8400, "y": 7200, "cubeLength": 300, "i": 28, "j": 24 } } ], "lastEvaluatedKey": "ff248cbb-e7ab-4510-817f-53f7d56cf3e0" } } update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "update_cube-price", "parameters": { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "cubesList": ["48_0", "51_0", "54_0"], "princeInCents": 500 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "updatedCubes": 3, "invalidCubes": 1, "invalidCubesList": [ "300_848483847938474000000" ] } } Previous Field Next Plays (Field-Calendar) Last updated 7 days ago Was this helpful?

paragraphs: Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches.
Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field.
check method parameters
The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes).
(optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes.
check method parameters
Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price)

pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Event Types API for defining in-game occurrences like goals, assists, and passes. Events are linked to fields with assigned point values and used for tracking live match activities that affect cube owners in specific zones.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new event type called 'pass' with 100 points assigned when detected on a cube
Lists all event types configured for a specific field with their associated points
Updates the point value for an existing event type from 100 to 1000 points

useCases: Define scoring rules for different soccer events like goals, assists, and passes
Assign point values to chess moves for tournament gameplay
Create custom event types for sport-specific actions (cricket wickets, basketball dunks)
Configure reward mechanisms for cube owners when events occur in their zones
Update event point values to balance gameplay and fan engagement

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Types
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type with fieldId, eventTypeName (e.g., 'pass', 'goal'), and eventTypePoints to define in-game occurrences

implementationSteps.title: Configure Point Values
implementationSteps.description: Set appropriate points for each event type based on significance (goals higher than passes)

implementationSteps.title: Update as Needed
implementationSteps.description: Use update_event-type to adjust point values or delete_event-type to remove unused event definitions

Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "eventTypes": [ { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "eventTypeId": "34339c99-43cf-4fe9-b632-4f8606e3c1bc", "eventTypeName": "pass", "eventTypePoints": "100" } ] } } update_event-type POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_event-type parameters * JSON check method parameters update_event-type Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption eventTypeId string The Event Type Id we want to update the assigned points. fieldId string The Field Id the event is linked to. eventTypePoints string The new number points assigned to the event (e.g. "1000") update_event-type API Call Example update-event-type API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "update_event-type", "parameters": { "eventTypdId": "34339c99-43cf-4fe9-b632-4f8606e3c1bc", "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "eventTypePoints": "1000" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Field API for creating virtual sports fields tokenized into interactive cube zones. Fields represent real-world venues like Camp Nou or Madison Square Garden with configurable dimensions and automatic cube generation upon creation.

codeExamples.description: Creates a football field representing Camp Nou with 120m x 80m dimensions and 3m x 3m cubes, automatically generating all cubes
Lists all created fields or retrieves specific field details by fieldId
Response containing the unique fieldId used to link cubes, calendars, and events

useCases: Create virtual representation of Real Madrid Stadium for fan engagement games
Tokenize Madison Square Garden into purchasable cube sections
Generate interactive football fields with 3x3 meter zones for live match gameplay
Build virtual cricket grounds with configurable dimensions for seasonal tournaments
Enable fans to own specific areas of iconic sports venues through NFTs

implementationSteps.title: Define Field Parameters
implementationSteps.description: Specify field name, sport type, dimensions in centimeters (fieldLength, fieldWidth), and cubeLength for zone size (e.g., 300 for 3x3m)

implementationSteps.title: Create Field
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field operation which automatically generates all cubes and returns fieldId for linking related objects

implementationSteps.title: Manage Field Lifecycle
implementationSteps.description: Use list_fields to retrieve field data and delete_field to remove fields (automatically deletes all associated cubes)

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page new_field Request Body new_field API Call Example list_fields Request Body list_fields API Call Example delete_field Request Body delete_field API Call Example Was this helpful? Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Field Represents a virtual sports field tokenized into interactive zones or sections ("CUBES"). It is the foundational canvas for all game interactions. Each field is typically linked to a real-world sports match. Examples: Real Madrid Stadium Madison Square Garden new_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_field parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Field name (e.g. Camp Nou) sport string Sport (e.g. Football) fieldLength number Field length in centimeters (e.g. 12000) fieldWidth number Field width in centimeters (e.g. 9000) cubeLength number The square size of a single cube (e.g. 300 for a cube of 3x3 meters) new_field API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "new_field", "parameters": { "name": "Camp Nou", "sport": "football", "fieldLength": 12000, "fieldWidth": 8000, "cubeLength": 300 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); new_field Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId , which will be used to link NFT Game Cube related objects. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field. Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" } } list_fields POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters list_fields Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id list_fields API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "list_fields", "parameters": { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); list_fields Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of fields you created or a single field if fieldId parameter is included in the call. Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "fields": [ { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "status": "ready", "data": { "name": "Camp Nou", "sport": "football", "fieldLength": 12000, "fieldWidth": 8000, "cubeLength": 300 } } ] } } delete_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_field parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id to delete. delete_field API Call Example delete_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "delete_field", "parameters": { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); delete_field Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. All the cubes created and assigned to the deleted field are also deleted. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field.

pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents Live Events API for recording real-time match events at specific field coordinates. Events can be captured manually via dashboard, automatically through ML models, or via application API, linking to cubes for scoring and rewards.

codeExamples.description: Records a live event at coordinates (45, 40) on the field during a scheduled match, triggering cube scoring
Successful response after recording a live event for cube reward processing

useCases: Record goals scored at specific field coordinates during live soccer matches
Capture chess moves automatically via AI-driven move recognition
Track fouls and plays manually through organizer dashboard interface
Enable machine learning models to detect and push game events in real-time
Award points to cube owners whose zones match the event location

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Event Data
implementationSteps.description: Gather matchId, fieldId, calendarId, event coordinates (x, y), and eventTypeId for the live event

implementationSteps.title: Submit Event
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event operation manually, via BWS ML models (AI goal recognition), or through your application during live matches

implementationSteps.title: Process Rewards
implementationSteps.description: System automatically maps event coordinates to corresponding cubes and assigns points to owners for scoring and prize eligibility

Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Live Events LiveEvent is the base API object used to record an in-match event that occurs at a specific coordinate ( x , y ) on a Field during a scheduled Match ( Calendar ). It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "new_event", "parameters": { "matchId": "e16d3f98-4eeb-44b9-837c-eddbbfe305ee", "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "event": { "coordinates": { "x": "45", "y": "40" }, "eventTypdId": "34339c99-43cf-4fe9-b632-4f8606e3c1bc" } } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Match objects in the FAN Game Cube system. Matches represent time-bound gameplay sessions tied to specific fields where events are tracked, points awarded, and fan interactions occur. Includes operations for creating, updating, listing, and deleting matches with team information and status tracking.

codeExamples.description: Create a new match for PSG vs Manchester City with scheduled start time and match details
List matches for a specific calendar or retrieve details of a single match by ID
API response returning the matchId for a newly created or updated match

useCases: Create football matches like PSG vs Manchester City with team flags and scheduling
Track match status changes from scheduled to playing to finished
Manage chess tournaments like Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura with event details
Update match information including descriptions, images, and pricing for cubes
List all matches in a calendar or retrieve specific match details for fan engagement

implementationSteps.title: Create a Match
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match operation with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, match name, team information, and base64 encoded images. The API returns a matchId for the created match.

implementationSteps.title: Update Match Details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match operation with matchId and calendarId to modify any field including status (scheduled, playing, finished, canceled), pricing, or team information.

implementationSteps.title: Track and List Matches
implementationSteps.description: Use list_matches to retrieve all matches for a calendar or specific match details. Images are automatically stored in IPFS and returned as URLs in the response.

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page new_match Request Body new_match API Call Example update_match Request Body update_match API Call Example list_matches Request Body list_matches API Call Example delete_match Request Body delete_match API Call Example Was this helpful? Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Match A Match is a time-bound, gameplay session tied to a specific Field, during which events are tracked, points are awarded, and fan interactions occur. It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters new_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to create a new NFT Game Cube match */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "new_match", "parameters": { "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "startTimeInMillis": 1737795600000, "name": "PSG vs Man City", "description": "Paris Saint-Germain will face Manchester City ...", "image": "9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQIAHA... " /* base64 encoded image string */ } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "matchId": "e16d3f98-4eeb-44b9-837c-eddbbfe305ee" } } update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters update_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to update match description only */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "new_match", "parameters": { "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "matchId": "e16d3f98-4eeb-44b9-837c-eddbbfe305ee", "description": "Paris Saint-Germain will face Manchester City ...", } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "matchId": "e16d3f98-4eeb-44b9-837c-eddbbfe305ee" } } list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "list_matches", "parameters": { "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "matchId": "e16d3f98-4eeb-44b9-837c-eddbbfe305ee" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "matches": [ { "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "matchId": "e16d3f98-4eeb-44b9-837c-eddbbfe305ee", "startTimeInMillis": "1737795600000", "status": "scheduled", "data": { "name": "PSG vs Man City", "description": "Paris Saint-Germain will face Manchester City ...", "image": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmQnRDsQPvNJ9RgT9bagHmCLQVxCyZxatkPpj3Avb1hsM8" } } ] } } delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "delete_match", "parameters": { "matchId": "e16d3f98-4eeb-44b9-837c-eddbbfe305ee", "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Field-Calendar relationships (Plays) in the NFT Game Cube solution. This models the temporal lifecycle where static field configurations host dynamic matches, tournaments, or rounds. Allows linking fields to calendars with descriptions and images for seasonal or event-based gameplay.

codeExamples.description: Create a field-calendar relationship for PSG's 2025-26 season with description and image
List all calendars associated with a specific field to view tournaments and seasons
Response showing field-calendar relationship with IPFS-stored image URL

useCases: Link PSG Champions League 2026 tournament to a football field configuration
Create New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season calendar on a basketball court field
Manage multiple seasons or tournaments on the same physical field layout
Track temporal progression of events on static sports ground configurations
Associate field cube layouts with specific tournament or season schedules

implementationSteps.title: Create Field-Calendar Link
implementationSteps.description: Use new_field-calendar operation with fieldId and calendarId to associate a field's spatial configuration with a temporal calendar. Include description and base64 image for season/tournament branding.

implementationSteps.title: List Associated Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_field-calendars with a fieldId to retrieve all calendars (seasons, tournaments) linked to that field. View descriptions and IPFS image URLs for each calendar.

implementationSteps.title: Delete Field-Calendar Relationship
implementationSteps.description: Use delete_field-calendar with both fieldId and calendarId to remove the association when a season ends or tournament concludes.

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page new_field-calendar Request Body new_field-calendar API Call Example list_field-calendars Request Body list_field-calendars API Call Example delete_field-calendar Request Body delete_field-calendar API Call Example Was this helpful? Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Plays (Field-Calendar) This relationship models the temporal lifecycle of a Field in the NFT Game Cube solution. A Field defines a sports ground's static, spatial configuration (like a 12×8 football pitch), while Calendar entries dynamically represent individual matches , tournaments , or rounds on that Field. Examples: PSG Champions League 2026 New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. new_field-calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field-calendar Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. calendarId string Calendar Id. description string The field calendar name (e.g. PSG 2025-26) image string An image base64 encoded. new_field-calendar API Call Example new_field-calendar API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "new_field-calendar", "parameters": { "fieldId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "description": "PSG 2025-26", "image": "9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQIAHA... " /* base64 encoded image string */ } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); new_field-calendar Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId and calendarId used to create the calendar and the related data, including the name and the image IPFS url. Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "data": { "name": "PSG 2025-26", "image": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmQnRDsQPvNJ9RgT9bagHmCLQVxCyZxatkPpj3Avb1hsM8" } } } list_field-calendars POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_field-calendars parameters JSON check method parameters list_field-calendars Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id list_field-calendars API Call Example list_field-calendars API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "list_field-calendars", "parameters": { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); list_field-calendars Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the calendars list linked to the provided field. Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "calendars": [ { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "data": { "name": "PSG 2025-26", "image": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmQnRDsQPvNJ9RgT9bagHmCLQVxCyZxatkPpj3Avb1hsM8" } } ] } } delete_field-calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field-calendar Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string The Filed Id we used to create the relationship we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id we used to create the relatinship to delete. delete_field-calendar API Call Example delete_field-calendar API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "delete_calendar", "parameters": { "fieldId": "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2", "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Prize objects in the FAN Game Cube system. Prizes are rewards (NFTs, tokens, merchandise, experiences) awarded based on cube performance in specific matches or seasons. Supports first, second, and third place prizes with rich descriptions, images, and HTML formatting for engagement.

codeExamples.description: Create a first place prize for a match with Phil Foden signed merchandise
List all prizes configured for a specific match including positions and details
Response showing match prizes with IPFS-stored images and position rankings

useCases: Award signed merchandise like autographed player frames to top cube performers
Offer VIP match tickets as prizes for winners of cube competitions
Provide Zoom calls or AMA sessions with athletes as engagement rewards
Distribute bonus BWS tokens to incentivize cube ownership and participation
Create tiered prize systems with first, second, and third place rewards

implementationSteps.title: Create Match Prize
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match-prize operation with matchId, calendarId, position (1, 2, or 3), prize name, description, and snapshot image. Optionally include HTML description and multiple images for rich presentation.

implementationSteps.title: Update Prize Details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match-prize with prizeId to modify prize information including descriptions, images, or HTML content. All parameters except IDs are optional for flexible updates.

implementationSteps.title: Manage Prize Lifecycle
implementationSteps.description: List prizes with list_match-prizes to view all rewards for a match. Delete prizes using delete_match-prize when they are no longer relevant or have been claimed.

pages.fullContent:
BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page new_match-prize Request Body new_match-prize API Call Example list_match-prizes Request Body list_match-prizes API Call Example update_match-prize Request Body update_match-prize API Call Example delete_match-prize Request Body delete_match-prize API Call Example Was this helpful? Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Prizes A Prize represents a reward asset or benefit that users can receive based on their CUBE performance, in a specific Match (or Season) . It can be a digital NFT, a token bonus, a physical good, or an experience, configurable by the solution owner to drive fan engagement and incentivize Cube ownership. Examples: Signed Merchandise VIP Match Tickets Zoom Call or AMA with an Athlete Bonus $BWS Tokens Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. new_match-prize POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match-prize parameters * JSON check method parameters new_match-prize Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId * string The Match Id we want to assign the prize. calendarId * string Calendar Id of the match. position * number 1, 2 or 3 for first, second and third prize. name * string The prize name (e.g. FODEN SIGNED FRAME) description * string Prize description (e.g. Phil Foden, known for his incredible..) snapshot * string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. htmlDescription string The HTML description of the prize (e.g.

The ...) image1 string An image base64 encoded string. image2 string An image base64 encoded string. image3 string An image base64 encoded string. new_match-prize API Call Example new_match-prize API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require('axios'); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution": "BWS.NFT.GameCube", "operation": "new_match-prize", "parameters": { "matchId": "e16d3f98-4eeb-44b9-837c-eddbbfe305ee", "calendarId": "8a7324f4-311d-43a8-a28f-87d9c424c354", "position": 1, "name": "FODEN SIGNED FRAME", "description": "Phil Foden, known for his incredible ...", "snapshot": "9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQIAHA... " /* base64 encoded image string */ } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method: 'post', maxBodyLength: Infinity, url: 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call', headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); new_match-prize Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the prizeId for the newly created prize. pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview and business model for the FAN Game Cube platform, which revolutionizes fan engagement through NFT-based digital field ownership. Covers multi-sport scalability (football, chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, etc.), cube allocation strategies, pricing models, and revenue generation. Explains how fans purchase field cubes, participate in real-time gameplay, and win rewards.
useCases: Tokenize football stadium sections into 500-2000 cubes for fan ownership and engagement
Create chess tournament engagement by tokenizing 64 chessboard squares for fans
Enable tennis match participation through tokenized court sections like baselines and service boxes
Generate revenue for sports clubs through cube sales at $10-$500+ per cube
Launch full-season or VIP match engagement campaigns with tradeable NFT cubes on secondary markets

implementationSteps.title: Define Cube Allocation Strategy
implementationSteps.description: Determine how many cubes to sell based on sport type (e.g., 500-2000 for football, 64 for chess). Assign premium pricing to strategic field areas like penalty boxes, center court, or goal zones.

implementationSteps.title: Integrate One-Click Purchase Widget
implementationSteps.description: Embed the JavaScript purchase widget into your club website for frictionless transactions. Fans can buy cubes using credit cards without complex payment gateways or special accounts.

implementationSteps.title: Launch and Engage Fans
implementationSteps.description: Publish your FAN Game Cube event with real-time mobile app integration. Fans manage cube collections, track matches, join leaderboards, and compete for prizes while clubs generate revenue and royalties from secondary sales.

Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here . Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Previous FAN Game Cube Next FAN Game Cube API Last updated 3 days ago Was this helpful?

pages.summary: X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform providing trusted X (Twitter) engagement metrics using the official X API. Helps crypto projects measure community impact and KOL profitability, enables KOL teams to showcase verifiable performance, and allows investors to discover projects with authentic community traction. Focuses on accuracy, transparency, and verifiable metrics.
useCases: Track crypto project X engagement and measure hired KOL campaign ROI with transparent metrics
Build KOL portfolios with verifiable performance data to get discovered by projects
Monitor KOL team performance and demonstrate client results with automated reports
Discover crypto investment opportunities with authentic community traction on xbot.ninja
Run community engagement competitions with leaderboards, prizes, and transparent scoring

pages.title: X Bot Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete command reference for X Bot organized by category including account tracking, keyword monitoring, cashtag tracking, mention management, exclusions, ignore lists, named filters, scheduled reports, customization, and credit management. Covers all operations for configuring X analytics, generating reports, and managing team or project tracking with detailed parameter descriptions and examples.

codeExamples.description: Add X accounts to track for monitoring posts and engagement metrics, optionally assigning to named filters
Track specific keywords and hashtags to monitor community discussions, using quotes for multi-word phrases
Schedule automated daily reports at 12:00 UTC, generate immediate reports, and customize engagement point weights

useCases: Configure tracking for project accounts, keywords, cashtags, and mentions to monitor X engagement
Schedule automated daily reports with custom timing for community updates and leaderboards
Customize performance scoring by adjusting point weights for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions
Create named filters for managing multiple clients, campaigns, or tracking scenarios independently
Generate branded reports with custom colors, titles, and project metadata for professional presentation

implementationSteps.title: Configure Tracking Filters
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts, /add_keywords, /add_cashtags, and /add_mentions to define what X content to track. Add exclusions with /add_exclusions to filter spam and irrelevant content.

implementationSteps.title: Set Up Automated Reports
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_schedule to configure daily report timing (up to 12-hour frequency). Customize scoring with /set_points and branding with /set_colors and title commands.

implementationSteps.title: Manage Advanced Features
implementationSteps.description: Create named filters for multiple tracking scenarios, hide internal accounts with /add_ignore_list, and purchase credits with /buy for continued usage. Use /recreate for immediate report generation.
